channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Samia Emphasizes Religious Freedom and National Unity
item.description: Dar es Salaam — President Samia Suluhu Hassan has stressed the importance of protecting religious freedom in Tanzania while maintaining national unity and peace. Speaking during the Eid Baraza as Musl...

item.title: Ex-Acting Accountant-General Nwabuoku Sentenced to 72 Years for N868 Million Fraud
item.description: For real, Nigeria don see another big corruption case waka finish. Chukwunyere Anamekwe Nwabuoku, wey be former Acting Accountant-General of the Federation, don get 72 years jail sentence after Federa...

item.title: Meloni Suffers Setback as Italian Voters Reject Reform Proposals
item.description: Omo, things get serious for Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni! Italian voters don chop her big time by rejecting her proposed justice reforms in a referendum. Almost 54 percent of voters said 'No' against...

item.title: Senator Pocock pushes for free public transport to ease cost-of-living pressures
item.description: Independent ACT senator David Pocock is spearheading a push in the federal Senate for free public transport across Australia in response to rising fuel prices. The motion, supported by the federal Gre...

item.title: Metropolitan Police investigate arson attack on Jewish ambulance service in Golders Green
item.description: Metropolitan Police are investigating an arson attack on the Jewish volunteer ambulance service Hatzola in Golders Green, north London, in which four ambulances were destroyed following a loud explosi...

item.title: Judge to Decide on $18M Settlement for 2015 Air Canada Halifax Crash Case
item.description: A Nova Scotia Supreme Court judge is expected to decide in June 2026 whether to approve an $18 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit over the 2015 crash landing of Air Canada Flight 624 in Hali...

item.title: KZN Steps Up Foot-and-Mouth Disease Vaccination Drive Across Districts
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal’s Agriculture and Rural Development MEC, Thembeni kaMadlopha-Mthethwa, has urged districts that have not yet received their Foot-and-Mouth Disease (FMD) vaccinations to make the necessar...

item.title: Nairobi Water Restores Supply After Komarock Pipeline Repairs
item.description: Nairobi City Water and Sewerage Company has confirmed that repairs on the Komarock pipeline are now complete, and water supply is gradually being restored to parts of eastern Nairobi. The pipeline ser...

item.title: Kaduna State Government boosts water supply and reforms sector
item.description: Kaduna State Government don yan say dem don dey make better progress for water sector under Governor Uba Sani administration. State Commissioner for Information, Ahmed Maiyaki, yan this one for statem...

item.title: Authorities destroy P4.56 billion worth of seized illegal drugs in Cavite operation
item.description: Sinunog at winasak ng mga awtoridad ang tinatayang P4.56 bilyon na halaga ng ilegal na droga sa Cavite bilang bahagi ng patuloy na kampanya ng gobyerno laban sa illegal drugs. Ang operasyong ito ay pi...

item.title: Saskatchewan premier responds to criticism over school construction planning and funding priorities
item.description: Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe is pushing back against criticism from the provincial NDP regarding the government’s approach to school construction projects. The debate centres on whether planned scho...

item.title: UN expert accuses Israel of systematic torture of Palestinians and global complicity
item.description: A United Nations expert has accused Israel of adopting torture as a state policy against Palestinians, saying that the international community has effectively given Tel Aviv a free hand to continue su...

item.title: Jason Momoa evacuates Hawaii home as severe flooding displaces thousands across Oahu
item.description: Actor Jason Momoa has confirmed that he and his family are safe after being forced to evacuate their home on Oahu, Hawaii, due to severe flooding caused by a major storm. The flooding, described as th...

item.title: Joshua Baraka surpasses Diamond Platnumz in Spotify monthly listeners across East Africa
item.description: Ugandan musician Joshua Baraka has achieved a major regional milestone by overtaking Tanzania’s celebrated Bongo Flava artist Diamond Platnumz on Spotify’s monthly listeners chart. According to Spotif...

item.title: Refugee Experiences Highlight Benefits of Settling in Regional Australian Towns
item.description: A recent study, 'Settling Well in Regional Australia', explored the experiences of 628 people from refugee backgrounds living in regional towns such as Albury, Wodonga, Mildura, Nhill, Orange, Cowra, ...

item.title: Traffic Affected by Flooding at Molo River Along Eldama Ravine-Molo Road
item.description: Traffic along the Eldama Ravine-Maji Mazuri-Molo Road has been seriously affected due to flooding at the Molo River. According to the Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA), heavy rains upstream ca...

item.title: Charlie Ang petitions for removal from Manila kidnapping case
item.description: The legal team of fugitive businessman Charlie “Atong” Ang has formally requested to be excluded from the ongoing Manila court case involving the disappearance of cockfight enthusiasts. Ang’s lawyer, ...

item.title: Islamabad High Court Cancels Two-Strikes Rule for Civil Service Promotions
item.description: The Islamabad High Court has struck down the 'two-strikes' rule that barred senior bureaucrats from promotion to BS-22 after being passed over twice. Justice Raja Inaam Ameen Minhas ruled that the Rec...

item.title: EU and Australia sign free trade deal and establish defence partnership
item.description: On March 24, 2026, the European Union and Australia signed the final text of a free trade agreement at the Australian Parliament House, marking a significant milestone in their economic and strategic ...

item.title: Zanzibar launches digital system to protect forests from illegal logging
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar has introduced a new digital technology aimed at monitoring and controlling illegal logging, particularly in sensitive mangrove and upland forest areas. The la...
